title: Count words, case-insensitive, ignoring punctuation
prompt: Implement `word_count(text)` in solution_stub.py. Return a dict mapping each word to its number of occurrences. Counting is case-insensitive (lowercase the words). Split on whitespace, then strip leading/trailing punctuation from each token. Punctuation INSIDE a word (like the apostrophe in don't) is kept. Empty tokens are ignored. The current version counts raw whitespace-split tokens, so punctuation and casing break the counts.
